Petition For Approval Of Minors Compromise
Off Calendar Aug-23-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for Plaintiff's motion for compromise of claim of Minor, Khalia B.

Dept. 505; Judge Gail Dekreon; Clerk: N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 1:47 p.m. and concluding at 1:52 p.m.

Petitioner and Guardian Ad Litem, Lindy Ford is present, represented by Neil Ferrera, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant GCI Inc. is not present.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents brief recitation of case.
Guardian ad Litem is examined by the Court.

The matter is submitted.
The Court finds the settlement is reasonable and the Guardian ad Litem confirms it is in the best interest of the claimant.

The court hereby APPROVES Plaintiff's Motion for Compromise of Claim for the settlement amount of $30,000.00 minus the following:

1. $7,500.00 for Attorney's Fees
2. $5,734.22 for Medical Expenses

Balance of settlement in the sum of $16,765.78 shall be deposited in a blocked account at Wells Fargo located at 7255 South Rainbow Boulevard, Las Vegas, NV 89119.

The Plaintiff is authorized and directed to execute all documents reasonably necessary to carry out the terms of the settlement.

Proposed Orders Approving Compromise of Claim or Action or Disposition of Proceeds of Judgment for Minor or Person with a Disability and to Deposit Funds in Blocked Account are signed and filed this date.

Court orders matter continued to November 17, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for progress report on the deposit of funds in a blocked account. (D505)

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-584353 WITH CASE CGC-19-580313
Off Calendar Sep-28-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Kelsey Fischer, Esq;(916) 379-3500; Dreyer Babich Buccola Wood Campora; kfischer@dbbwc.com; for Plaintiff Lindy Marie Ford et al
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Court reporter,
No other appearances

Plaintiff contests the Court's tentative ruling.

Defendants counsel is not present at the time this matter is called. Court ask plaintiff counsel to contact opposing counsel to let them know that matter is ready to be heard and the matter is passed at 9:31 a.m. Matter recalled at 9:44 a.m. Ms. Fischer states that she was able to reach Mr. Kevin Hermanson who opposed the motion to consolidate on behlaf of Defendant Liberty Mutual. Mr. Hermanson alleges that he did not recieve the email contesting the Court's tentative ruling and that he is unable to appear this morning. Court moves forward with hearing.

Palintiff's counsel ARGUES. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

PLAINTIFFS LINDY FORD, KHALIA BROWN'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-584353 WITH CASE CGC-19-580313.

Plaintiff's motion to consolidate this action with Ford v. Liberty Mutual Insurance Company., No. CGC-20-584353, is denied. The two actions arise out of two unrelated motor vehicle accidents separated by more than seven months, and will require different witnesses and other proof. While there may be some overlap in plaintiff's healthcare providers, expert witnesses and questions of causation, that is not sufficient to warrant consolidation of otherwise unrelated actions that present distinct factual and legal issues. Moreover, plaintiff and Liberty Mutual are parties to an arbitration agreement, while there is none in the instant action. Liberty Mutual's request in its opposition that this Court order the parties in the second case to arbitration or stay the arbitration pending the outcome of the instant litigation is misplaced; it must file a separate motion to compel arbitration and/or stay.

Counsel for the respondent Liberty Mutual is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC3.1312.

*10:55 a.m. After hearing, Kevin Hermanson, Esq., for Liberty Mutual Insurance appears on ZOOM. Mr. Hermanson states that he did not receive notice that oposing counsel contested the Court's tentative ruing. court states that the email received by Plaintiffs counsel was sent at 2:04 pm to SantanaVierra@libertymutual.com and 2 additional counsel for Lewis Brisbois Bisgard & Smith. Court informs counsel that the Court's tentative ruling has been adopted.**

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)
